message.content:
{
  "round": 3,
  "parent_reward_id": "round2_candidate0_component_recomposition",
  "selected_candidate_id": "round3_candidate2_local_repair",
  "mutation_family": "local_repair",
  "observed_outcome": {
    "coarse_result": "partial_progress_available",
    "main_failure_remaining": "no success_like trajectories",
    "main_success_signal": "selected candidate produces only partial_progress trajectories, eliminating early failures and low_progress_survival in its own evaluation"
  },
  "failure_repair_outcome": {
    "failure_before": "no success_like trajectories; safe_landing_bonus never fires due to overly strict condition",
    "repair_attempt": "restructured safe_landing_bonus to provide partial credit for sub-conditions and added intermediate rewards for leg contact and stability near pad",
    "outcome_after": "candidate generates 50 partial_progress trajectories but still no success_like achieved; safe_landing_bonus remains insufficient to trigger final terminal condition"
  },
  "lesson": "Local repair that provides intermediate rewards for progress toward terminal condition can eliminate early failures and produce consistent partial progress, but bridging the gap to success_like requires aligning reward with the exact terminal state rather than intermediate approximations; pure partial-progress dominance does not imply success readiness.",
  "future_guidance": [
    "preserve approach_progress component as it drives partial_progress",
    "avoid treating partial_progress as success_like in preference construction",
    "strengthen terminal condition reward with progressive relaxation or shaping to encourage the final landing step",
    "consider using curriculum learning or gradual tightening of safe_landing condition to guide policy from partial_progress to success_like"
  ],
  "archive_update": {
    "add_to_elite_archive": true,
    "rollback_triggered": false,
    "next_parent_reward_id": "round3_candidate2_local_repair",
    "reason": "Candidate selected for long training and added to elite archive; no rollback needed as conditions not met."
  },
  "uncertainties": [
    "No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed",
    "Selection based on weak pairwise evidence, conclusions may not generalize to success behavior",
    "True success may require a fundamentally different reward structure not yet explored"
  ]
}
